title: APR Analytics Metrics
description: Per-workflow analytics file at .apr/analytics/<workflow>/metrics.json. v1.1 extends v1.0 (fzi.1) with trust/provenance signals from bd-2ic (manifest hash, ACK status, files-report verification, ledger presence). See docs/schemas/metrics-schema.md for prose spec.

schema_version.description: Stable identifier. 1.0.0 == fzi.1 (initial); 1.1.0 adds trust[] per round (bd-2ic); 1.2.0 adds execution[] per round (bd-6rw).

trust.description: bd-2ic trust/provenance signals. Optional in v1.0.0, recommended in v1.1.0.

manifest_present.description: True iff the run had a manifest preamble (bd-3i5). When false, the model had no explicit binding to file hashes.

manifest_hash.description: sha256 of the manifest section, copied from the run ledger (bd-246). Null if the manifest was not present or not recorded.

prompt_hash.description: sha256 of the FINAL assembled prompt (manifest + template), copied from the run ledger.

ack_present.description: True iff the model emitted an ACK block (bd-34z).

ack_complete.description: True iff every required doc appears in the ACK block.

ack_matches_manifest.description: True iff ACK entries' sha256 + bytes match the recorded manifest.

files_report_supported.description: True iff the installed Oracle exposes --files-report (bd-1tl). Null if detection wasn't attempted.

files_report_ok.description: True iff oracle's files-report matched the expected attachment set. Null when files_report_supported is false/null.

files_report_mismatch.description: Per-class mismatch detail when files_report_ok is false.

ledger_present.description: True iff .apr/rounds/<wf>/round_<N>.meta.json exists for this round (bd-246).

ledger_schema_version.description: Value of `schema_version` in the round's ledger (e.g. `apr_run_ledger.v1`).

secret_detected_count.description: Number of secret-scan findings for this round (bd-1eq). Null if the scan didn't run.

redaction_count.description: Number of redactions applied by lib/redact.sh before the prompt was sent (bd-3ut). Null if redaction wasn't enabled.

low_trust.description: Derived flag, true iff ANY of: manifest_present==false, ack_present==false, ack_matches_manifest==false, files_report_ok==false, ledger_present==false. Consumers can branch on this single field for dashboard rendering.

execution.description: bd-6rw execution-signals block (schema_version >= 1.2.0). Surfaces busy/retry/queue dynamics so apr stats can flag rounds whose CORRECTNESS is fine but whose RUNTIME was unhealthy.

busy_wait_events.description: Number of times oracle busy was detected and waited (bd-3pu). Mirrors execution.busy_wait_count from the run ledger (bd-246).

busy_wait_total_ms.description: Cumulative milliseconds spent in busy backoff (bd-3du). Mirrors the ledger field. Useful as a single integer to bucket runs into healthy / degraded / pathological.

retry_attempts.description: Number of full oracle-invocation retries within this run_id (non-busy retries via the generic backoff). Mirrors execution.retries_count from the run ledger.

retry_exit_codes.description: Oracle exit codes from EACH retry attempt, in attempt order. Useful for classifying retry storms (all `2` -> oracle_error; mixed -> network glitches).

queue_run_id.description: queue entry_id if this round was dispatched via `apr queue run` (bd-3fn). Null when the round was an interactive `apr run`.

queued_at.description: Wall-clock when the entry was enqueued. Null for non-queued rounds.

started_at.description: Wall-clock when oracle invocation began (mirrors run ledger started_at).

completed_at.description: Wall-clock when the round reached its terminal state (mirrors run ledger finished_at).

duration_ms.description: Wall-clock duration in milliseconds (mirrors run ledger duration_ms).

outcome_class.description: Coarse outcome classification aligned with the error taxonomy (bd-3tj). Derived from the run ledger's outcome.code (or from the queue event log when no ledger exists). `unknown` is the default when neither source is available.

degraded_runtime.description: Derived flag, true iff ANY of: busy_wait_events > 0 AND busy_wait_total_ms > APR_DEGRADED_BUSY_MS_THRESHOLD (default 60000), retry_attempts > 0, outcome_class IN {busy_timeout, oracle_error, network_error}. Consumers can branch on this single field for dashboard rendering (separate from `trust.low_trust`).
